To overcome this limitation, we propose Fast3Dcache, a training-free geometry-aware caching framework that accelerates 3D diffusion inference while preserving geometric fidelity. Our method introduces a Predictive Caching Scheduler Constraint (PCSC) to dynamically determine cache quotas according to voxel stabilization patterns and a Spatiotemporal Stability Criterion (SSC) to select stable features for reuse based on velocity magnitude and acceleration criterion. Comprehensive experiments show that Fast3Dcache accelerates inference significantly, achieving up to a 27.12% speed-up and a 54.83% reduction in FLOPs, with minimal degradation in geometric quality as measured by Chamfer Distance (2.48%) and F-Score (1.95%).
comment: Accepted by CVPR 2026; Project page: https://fast3dcache-agi.github.io

light_analysis.overview: Fast3Dcache是一个无需训练的几何感知缓存框架，显著加速3D扩散模型的推理过程，同时保持几何一致性。
light_analysis.motivation: 扩散模型在2D图像、视频和3D形状生成中表现出色，但推理过程因迭代去噪而计算昂贵。现有基于缓存的方法在2D和视频生成中能有效重用计算以加速，但直接应用于3D扩散模型会严重破坏几何一致性，因为缓存潜在特征中的微小数值误差会累积，导致结构伪影和拓扑不一致。这一问题对3D建模、虚拟现实等应用至关重要，需要高效且高保真的生成方法。
light_analysis.method: 本文提出Fast3Dcache框架，核心包括预测缓存调度器约束（PCSC）和时空稳定性准则（SSC）。PCSC根据体素稳定模式动态确定缓存配额，以优化资源分配；SSC基于速度大小和加速标准选择稳定特征进行重用，避免几何失真。该方法无需额外训练，直接集成到现有3D扩散模型中，通过智能缓存减少冗余计算。
light_analysis.result: 实验显示Fast3Dcache显著加速推理，实现高达27.12%的速度提升和54.83%的FLOPs减少。几何质量评估中，Chamfer Distance变化仅为2.48%，F-Score变化为1.95%，表明在保持高几何保真度的同时有效提升效率，相比基线方法有明显改进。
light_analysis.conclusion: Fast3Dcache的主要贡献是开发了一个无需训练的缓存框架，解决3D扩散模型中缓存引起的几何一致性问题。研究具有学术价值，为3D生成效率优化提供了新思路；实际应用中，能加速3D内容创建，降低计算成本。局限性可能在于对特定场景的适用性，未来工作可探索扩展至更多3D任务或结合其他优化技术。

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Detection of Mental Health Conditions and Cyberbullying from Social Media
abstract: Mental health challenges and cyberbullying are increasingly prevalent in digital spaces, necessitating scalable and interpretable detection systems. This paper introduces a unified multiclass classification framework for detecting ten distinct mental health and cyberbullying categories from social media data. We curate datasets from Twitter and Reddit, implementing a rigorous "split-then-balance" pipeline to train on balanced data while evaluating on a realistic, held-out imbalanced test set. We conducted a comprehensive evaluation comparing traditional lexical models, hybrid approaches, and several end-to-end fine-tuned transformers. Our results demonstrate that end-to-end fine-tuning is critical for performance, with the domain-adapted MentalBERT emerging as the top model, achieving an accuracy of 0.92 and a Macro F1 score of 0.76, surpassing both its generic counterpart and a zero-shot LLM baseline. Grounded in a comprehensive ethical analysis, we frame the system as a human-in-the-loop screening aid, not a diagnostic tool. To support this, we introduce a hybrid SHAPLLM explainability framework and present a prototype dashboard ("Social Media Screener") designed to integrate model predictions and their explanations into a practical workflow for moderators. Our work provides a robust baseline, highlighting future needs for multi-label, clinically-validated datasets at the critical intersection of online safety and computational mental health.
comment: Best Paper Award at the AAAI-26 Bridge Program on AI for Medicine and Healthcare. Published in Proceedings of the Second AAAI Bridge Program on AI for Medicine and Healthcare, PMLR 317:15-26, 2026. Paper URL: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v317/ajayi26a.html

light_analysis.overview: 本论文提出了一种结合领域适应MentalBERT模型和混合SHAPLLM可解释性框架的统一多类分类方法，用于检测社交媒体中的心理健康和网络欺凌问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 心理健康挑战和网络欺凌在数字空间日益普遍，迫切需要可扩展且可解释的检测系统。现有方法可能缺乏统一的框架来处理多种类别，或解释性不足以支持实际决策。本研究旨在开发一种机器学习系统，不仅能准确识别问题，还能提供透明解释，辅助版主进行筛查而非诊断，从而提升在线安全与心理健康干预的效率和可靠性。摘要未明确说明具体现有方法缺点，但推断传统模型可能性能有限。
light_analysis.method: 研究方法包括构建统一的多类分类框架，用于检测十种心理健康和网络欺凌类别。通过收集Twitter和Reddit数据，采用'split-then-balance'管道平衡训练集并在真实不平衡测试集上评估。核心是端到端微调transformer模型，比较了传统词汇模型、混合方法及多种transformer模型。关键创新在于使用领域适应的MentalBERT模型，并结合SHAP与LLM的混合解释框架开发了'Social Media Screener'原型仪表板，以集成预测和解释到实际工作流中。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，端到端微调的MentalBERT模型表现最佳，准确率达到0.92，Macro F1分数为0.76，显著超过通用transformer模型和零样本LLM基线。这表明领域适应对性能至关重要，验证了所提框架的有效性。与基线方法相比，该方法在准确率和F1分数上均有显著提升，为心理健康和网络欺凌检测提供了强有力的基准结果。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究提供了一个稳健的基线框架，结合了高性能模型和可解释技术，强调系统作为人在环筛查辅助工具的应用价值。贡献在于展示了领域适应和解释框架的实用性，并指出未来需要开发多标签和临床验证的数据集，以进一步推动在线安全与计算心理健康领域的交叉研究，提升系统可靠性和实用性。

title: MimiCAT: Mimic with Correspondence-Aware Cascade-Transformer for Category-Free 3D Pose Transfer
abstract: 3D pose transfer aims to transfer the pose-style of a source mesh to a target character while preserving both the target's geometry and the source's pose characteristic. Existing methods are largely restricted to characters with similar structures and fail to generalize to category-free settings (e.g., transferring a humanoid's pose to a quadruped). The key challenge lies in the structural and transformation diversity inherent in distinct character types, which often leads to mismatched regions and poor transfer quality. To address these issues, we first construct a million-scale pose dataset across hundreds of distinct characters. We further propose MimiCAT, a cascade-transformer model designed for category-free 3D pose transfer. Instead of relying on strict one-to-one correspondence mappings, MimiCAT leverages semantic keypoint labels to learn a novel soft correspondence that enables flexible many-to-many matching across characters. The pose transfer is then formulated as a conditional generation process, in which the source transformations are first projected onto the target through soft correspondence matching and subsequently refined using shape-conditioned representations. Extensive qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate that MimiCAT generalizes plausible poses across diverse character morphologies, surpassing prior approaches restricted to narrow-category transfer (e.g., humanoid-to-humanoid).
comment: Accepted to CVPR 2026. Project page: https://mimicat3d.github.io/

light_analysis.overview: MimiCAT通过软对应匹配和级联transformer模型，实现了类别无关的3D姿态转移，解决了现有方法在处理结构差异大角色时的泛化问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 3D姿态转移旨在将源网格的姿态风格转移到目标角色，同时保持目标几何和源姿态特征。现有方法主要局限于结构相似的角色（如人形到人形），难以处理类别无关设置（如人形到四足动物），导致匹配区域不匹配和转移质量低下。随着计算机图形学和虚拟现实应用的发展，通用3D姿态转移对动画制作、游戏角色动画等领域至关重要，因此需要开发新方法来克服结构多样性带来的挑战。
light_analysis.method: 本研究首先构建了一个大规模数据集，包含数百万姿态样本，覆盖数百个不同角色。提出的MimiCAT模型采用级联transformer架构，专为类别无关3D姿态转移设计。核心创新是利用语义关键点标签学习软对应匹配，替代传统严格一对一对应，实现灵活的多对多跨角色匹配。姿态转移被表述为条件生成过程：先通过软对应匹配将源姿态变换投影到目标，再利用形状条件表示进行细化，以保持目标几何特征。
light_analysis.result: 广泛的定性和定量实验表明，MimiCAT在多样性角色形态上能生成视觉上合理的姿态，超越先前局限于窄类别转移的方法（如人形到人形）。实验结果验证了该方法在类别无关设置下的有效泛化能力，解决了现有方法的结构限制，但摘要未明确说明具体性能指标如准确率或效率改进数据。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是提出了MimiCAT框架，通过软对应匹配和级联transformer实现了类别无关的3D姿态转移，克服了结构多样性带来的挑战。这一方法具有重要学术价值，推动了计算机图形学中通用姿态建模的发展，并提升了在动画、游戏等领域的应用潜力。未来工作可优化软对应学习，或扩展到更复杂的3D内容生成任务中。

title: Uni-DAD: Unified Distillation and Adaptation of Diffusion Models for Few-step Few-shot Image Generation
abstract: Diffusion models (DMs) produce high-quality images, yet their sampling remains costly when adapted to new domains. Distilled DMs are faster but typically remain confined within their teacher's domain. Thus, fast and high-quality generation for novel domains relies on two-stage pipelines: Adapt-then-Distill or Distill-then-Adapt. However, both add design complexity and often degrade quality or diversity. We introduce Uni-DAD, a single-stage pipeline that unifies DM distillation and adaptation. It couples two training signals: (i) a dual-domain distribution-matching distillation (DMD) objective that guides the student toward the distributions of the source teacher and a target teacher, and (ii) a multi-head generative adversarial network (GAN) loss that encourages target realism across multiple feature scales. The source domain distillation preserves diverse source knowledge, while the multi-head GAN stabilizes training and reduces overfitting, especially in few-shot regimes. The inclusion of a target teacher facilitates adaptation to more structurally distant domains. We evaluate Uni-DAD on two comprehensive benchmarks for few-shot image generation (FSIG) and subject-driven personalization (SDP) using diffusion backbones. It delivers better or comparable quality to state-of-the-art (SoTA) adaptation methods even with less than 4 sampling steps, and often surpasses two-stage pipelines in quality and diversity. Code: https://github.com/yaramohamadi/uni-DAD.
comment: Accepted at IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2026

light_analysis.overview: Uni-DAD 提出了一种单阶段流水线，统一扩散模型的蒸馏和适应，用于在少步少样本场景下实现高效、高质量的图像生成。
light_analysis.motivation: 研究动机在于扩散模型虽能生成高质量图像，但采样成本高，特别是在适应新领域时。蒸馏技术可加速生成，但通常局限于源领域，导致在实际应用中依赖复杂的两阶段流水线（如先适应后蒸馏或先蒸馏后适应）。这些方法增加了设计复杂性，并常常损害生成质量或多样性，因此迫切需要一种简化且有效的方法，以在快速生成的同时保持或提升性能。
light_analysis.method: Uni-DAD 采用单阶段方法，结合两种训练信号：双领域分布匹配蒸馏（DMD）目标指导学生模型同时对齐源教师和目标教师的分布，以及多头部生成对抗网络（GAN）损失在多特征尺度上确保生成图像的真实性。关键创新在于统一蒸馏和适应，利用扩散模型作为骨干，源域蒸馏保留多样性知识，多头部GAN减少过拟合，特别适用于少样本场景。
light_analysis.result: 在少样本图像生成（FSIG）和主题驱动个性化（SDP）的基准测试中，Uni-DAD 在少于4个采样步骤的情况下，性能与最先进适应方法相当或更优。它常超越两阶段流水线，在生成质量和多样性方面表现出色，摘要未提供具体数值指标，但强调了其在效率和效果上的优势，表明该方法能在低采样成本下提升生成性能。
light_analysis.conclusion: Uni-DAD 的主要贡献是开发了一种统一的单阶段流水线，有效结合扩散模型的蒸馏和适应，解决了现有方法复杂性和性能下降问题。学术上，它为快速图像生成提供了新思路；实际应用中，适用于少样本图像生成和个性化任务，具有广泛潜力。摘要未明确说明局限性，但未来工作可能涉及扩展到更多领域或进一步优化。

title: Bayesian Calibration of Engine-out NOx Models for Engine-to-Engine Transferability
abstract: Accurate prediction of engine-out NOx is essential for meeting stringent emissions regulations and optimizing engine performance. Traditional approaches rely on models trained on data from a small number of engines, which can be insufficient in generalizing across an entire population of engines due to sensor biases and variations in input conditions. In real world applications, these models require tuning or calibration to maintain acceptable error tolerance when applied to other engines. This highlights the need for models that can adapt with minimal adjustments to accommodate engine-to-engine variability and sensor discrepancies. While previous studies have explored machine learning methods for predicting engine-out NOx, these approaches often fail to generalize reliably across different engines and operating environments. To address these issues, we propose a Bayesian calibration framework that combines Gaussian processes (GP) with approximate Bayesian computation to infer and correct sensor biases. Starting with a pre-trained model developed using nominal engine data, our method identifies engine specific sensor biases and recalibrates predictions accordingly. By incorporating these inferred biases, our approach generates posterior predictive distributions for engine-out NOx on unseen test data, achieving high accuracy without retraining the model. Our results demonstrate that this transferable modeling approach significantly improves the accuracy of predictions compared to conventional non-adaptive GP models, effectively addressing engine-to-engine variability and improving model generalizability.
comment: Accepted at International Journal of Engine Research

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出了一种结合高斯过程和近似贝叶斯计算的贝叶斯校准框架，以提高发动机排放NOx模型的跨发动机泛化性。
light_analysis.motivation: 准确预测发动机排放NOx对于满足严格排放法规和优化发动机性能至关重要。传统方法依赖基于少量发动机数据训练的模型，由于传感器偏差和输入条件变化，这些模型难以泛化到整个发动机群体，在实际应用中需要频繁校准以维持可接受的误差容忍度。现有机器学习方法往往无法可靠地跨不同发动机和操作环境泛化，突出了开发能够适应发动机间变异性并减少校准需求的模型的必要性。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出一个贝叶斯校准框架，结合高斯过程（GP）与近似贝叶斯计算（ABC）来推断和校正传感器偏差。该方法起始于使用标称发动机数据预训练的模型，识别发动机特定的传感器偏差，并相应重新校准预测。通过整合这些推断出的偏差，生成发动机排放NOx的后验预测分布，实现高精度预测而无需重新训练模型。关键创新点在于利用近似贝叶斯计算来处理不确定性，并基于贝叶斯推断实现模型自适应校准。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，这种可转移的建模方法显著提高了预测准确性。与传统的非自适应高斯过程模型相比，该方法有效处理了发动机间变异性，并改善了模型的泛化能力。摘要未提供具体性能指标数据，但指出实现了高精度预测，且无需重新训练模型，这有助于减少跨发动机应用时的校准成本和时间。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究的主要贡献是开发了一个贝叶斯校准框架，用于增强发动机排放NOx模型的跨发动机可转移性，通过推断传感器偏差和重新校准预测，减少了模型在不同发动机上的校准需求。学术价值在于结合贝叶斯方法和机器学习，推动了排放预测模型的泛化研究；实际应用价值在于有助于满足排放法规和优化发动机性能控制。潜在局限性可能包括对传感器数据的依赖性或扩展到更复杂操作环境的挑战，但摘要未明确说明未来工作方向。

title: Pharos-ESG: A Framework for Multimodal Parsing, Contextual Narration, and Hierarchical Labeling of ESG Report
abstract: Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles are reshaping the foundations of global financial gover- nance, transforming capital allocation architectures, regu- latory frameworks, and systemic risk coordination mecha- nisms. However, as the core medium for assessing corpo- rate ESG performance, the ESG reports present significant challenges for large-scale understanding, due to chaotic read- ing order from slide-like irregular layouts and implicit hier- archies arising from lengthy, weakly structured content. To address these challenges, we propose Pharos-ESG, a uni- fied framework that transforms ESG reports into structured representations through multimodal parsing, contextual nar- ration, and hierarchical labeling. It integrates a reading-order modeling module based on layout flow, hierarchy-aware seg- mentation guided by table-of-contents anchors, and a multi- modal aggregation pipeline that contextually transforms vi- sual elements into coherent natural language. The framework further enriches its outputs with ESG, GRI, and sentiment labels, yielding annotations aligned with the analytical de- mands of financial research. Extensive experiments on anno- tated benchmarks demonstrate that Pharos-ESG consistently outperforms both dedicated document parsing systems and general-purpose multimodal models. In addition, we release Aurora-ESG, the first large-scale public dataset of ESG re- ports, spanning Mainland China, Hong Kong, and U.S. mar- kets, featuring unified structured representations of multi- modal content, enriched with fine-grained layout and seman- tic annotations to better support ESG integration in financial governance and decision-making.

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出Pharos-ESG框架，通过多模态解析、上下文叙述和层次标注，将混乱的ESG报告转化为结构化表示，以支持金融研究和决策自动化。
light_analysis.motivation: ESG（环境、社会和治理）原则在全球金融治理中日益重要，但ESG报告作为评估公司表现的核心媒介，存在显著挑战。这些报告通常采用类似幻灯片的非规则布局，导致阅读顺序混乱；内容冗长且弱结构化，隐含复杂层次，使得大规模自动化分析困难。现有方法难以有效处理这种多模态文档的复杂性和隐含结构，阻碍了金融治理中的资本分配和风险管理，因此迫切需要创新技术来解析和结构化这些报告，以便更好地支持金融研究和决策。
light_analysis.method: Pharos-ESG框架整合了三个核心模块：首先，基于布局流的阅读顺序建模模块，通过分析文档视觉布局恢复逻辑阅读顺序；其次，利用目录锚点引导的层次感知分割，识别并结构化报告的内容层次；最后，多模态聚合管道将视觉元素（如图表和图形）上下文转化为连贯的自然语言描述。框架输出进一步添加ESG、GRI（全球报告倡议）标准和情感标签，生成结构化表示，对齐金融研究的需求，以支持自动化分析和集成应用。
light_analysis.result: 在标注基准上的广泛实验表明，Pharos-ESG框架一致优于专用文档解析系统和通用多模态模型，展示了其在处理ESG报告方面的优越性能。具体指标未在摘要中提供，但实验证实了该框架在多模态解析和结构化输出上的有效性。此外，研究团队发布了Aurora-ESG数据集，这是首个大规模公开ESG报告数据集，覆盖中国大陆、香港和美国市场，提供统一的多模态内容结构化表示和细粒度布局与语义注释，进一步支持金融治理中的ESG集成和决策制定。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献在于提出Pharos-ESG框架和发布Aurora-ESG数据集，解决了ESG报告的结构化解析难题。学术价值在于推动了多模态文档理解技术，特别是针对非规则布局和弱结构内容的应用；实际应用价值体现在促进金融治理中的ESG自动化分析和决策支持。未来工作可扩展数据集到更多市场，优化标签系统，或探索在更广泛的文档理解任务中的应用，尽管摘要未明确说明具体方向，但为后续研究奠定了基础。

title: Enhancing Nuclear Reactor Core Simulation through Data-Based Surrogate Models
abstract: In recent years, there has been an increasing need for Nuclear Power Plants (NPPs) to improve flexibility in order to match the rapid growth of renewable energies. The Operator Assistance Predictive System (OAPS) developed by Framatome addresses this problem through Model Predictive Control (MPC). In this work, we aim to improve MPC methods through data-driven simulation schemes. Thus, from a set of nonlinear stiff ordinary differential equations (ODEs), this paper introduces two surrogate models acting as alternative simulation schemes to enhance nuclear reactor core simulation. We show that both data-driven and physics-informed models can rapidly integrate complex dynamics, with a very low computational time (up to 1000x time reduction).

light_analysis.overview: 本文通过引入数据驱动和物理信息的代理模型，显著提升了核反应堆核心仿真的计算效率和速度。
light_analysis.motivation: 随着可再生能源的快速增长，核电站需要提高运营灵活性以匹配电网需求。现有运营商辅助预测系统使用模型预测控制方法，但基于非线性刚性常微分方程的仿真计算成本高、速度慢，限制了实时决策和灵活性优化。因此，本研究旨在开发高效的仿真方案，解决计算效率瓶颈，支持核电站的快速响应能力。
light_analysis.method: 本研究基于一组非线性刚性常微分方程，提出了两种代理模型作为替代仿真方案：数据驱动模型和物理信息模型。这些模型通过从数据中学习或结合物理约束，快速近似核反应堆核心的复杂动态，关键创新在于将代理模型集成到模型预测控制框架中以加速仿真。具体数据集和模型架构摘要未明确说明。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，两种代理模型都能高效集成复杂动态，计算时间显著降低，最高可达原始方法的1000倍。这大大提升了仿真实时性，为核反应堆核心的快速模拟提供了可行方案，与基于传统常微分方程的仿真相比，在效率上有显著改进。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究通过数据驱动和物理信息的代理模型，有效提高了核反应堆核心仿真的效率，支持了核电站灵活运营需求。学术上为仿真加速提供了新方法，实际中具有优化控制的应用价值。局限性可能包括模型精度或泛化能力，未来工作可进一步优化模型或扩展到其他工业领域。

title: CastMind: An Interaction-Driven Agentic Reasoning Framework for Cognition-Inspired Time Series Forecasting
abstract: Time series forecasting plays a crucial role in decision-making across many real-world applications. Despite substantial progress, most existing methods still treat forecasting as a static, single-pass regression problem. In contrast, human experts form predictions through iterative reasoning that integrates temporal features, domain knowledge, case-based references, and supplementary context, with continuous refinement. In this work, we propose CastMind, an interaction-driven agentic reasoning framework that enables accurate time series forecasting with training-free large language models. CastMind reformulates forecasting as an expert-like process and organizes it into a multi-stage workflow involving context preparation, reasoning-based generation, and reflective evaluation, transforming forecasting from a single-pass output into a multi-turn, autonomous interaction process. To support diverse perspectives commonly considered by human experts, we develop a lightweight toolkit comprising a feature set, a knowledge base, a case library, and a contextual pool that provides external support for LLM-based reasoning. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks show that CastMind generally outperforms representative baselines. Code is available at this repository: https://github.com/SkyeGT/CastMind .

light_analysis.overview: CastMind是一个交互驱动的代理推理框架，通过免训练大语言模型将时间序列预测转化为多阶段、类似专家的过程。
light_analysis.motivation: 时间序列预测在许多现实应用中至关重要，但现有方法大多将其视为静态、单次的回归问题，忽略了人类专家通过迭代推理整合时间特征、领域知识和案例参考的认知过程。这种简化可能导致预测不准确或缺乏灵活性，无法适应复杂的时间动态。本研究旨在弥补这一不足，提出一个更接近人类专家推理的预测框架，以提升预测质量和决策支持能力。
light_analysis.method: CastMind框架重新定义预测为多阶段工作流，包括上下文准备、推理生成和反思评估。核心创新在于引入交互驱动的代理推理，利用免训练大型语言模型，无需额外训练。此外，开发了轻量级工具包，包含特征集、知识库、案例库和上下文池，为LLM提供外部支持，使预测过程能够进行多轮自主交互，模仿专家决策。
light_analysis.result: 在多个基准数据集上的广泛实验表明，CastMind框架在时间序列预测任务上通常优于代表性基线方法。摘要未明确说明具体性能指标如准确率提升百分比，但实验结果验证了其有效性。与静态单次方法相比，CastMind通过交互推理和外部资源集成，展现了更好的预测性能，证明其能够有效捕捉时间动态和上下文信息。
light_analysis.conclusion: CastMind的主要贡献是提出一个交互驱动的代理推理框架，将时间序列预测从单次输出转变为多轮、自主的交互过程。这模仿了人类专家的认知方式，提高了预测的准确性和适应性。研究具有重要学术价值，融合了认知科学和人工智能，推动了预测方法向更智能的方向发展。潜在局限性可能包括对工具包的依赖和计算复杂性；未来工作可以优化框架效率或扩展应用到其他领域。

title: Quantification and object perception in Multimodal Large Language Models and human linguistic cognition
abstract: Quantification has been proven to be a particularly difficult linguistic phenomenon for (Multimodal) Large Language Models (MLLMs). However, given that quantification interfaces with the logic, pragmatic, and numerical domains, the exact reasons for the poor performance are still unclear. This paper looks at three key features of human quantification shared cross-linguistically that have remained so far unexplored in the (M)LLM literature: the ordering of quantifiers into scales, the ranges of use and prototypicality, and the biases inherent in the human approximate number system. The aim is to determine how these features are encoded in the models' architecture, how they may differ from humans, and whether the results are affected by the type of model (thinking vs. instruct) and the language under investigation. Results show that although thinking models showed a high accuracy in the numerosity estimation task and in the organization of quantifiers into scales, there are still key differences between humans and LLMs across all model types, particularly in terms of ranges of use and prototypicality values. This work, thus, paves the way for addressing the nature of MLLMs as semantic and pragmatic agents, while the cross-linguistic lens can elucidate whether their abilities are robust and stable across different languages.

light_analysis.overview: 本文通过分析人类量化的三个关键跨语言特征，揭示了多模态大语言模型在量化任务上与人类在使用范围和原型性上的差异，为理解模型作为语义和语用 agent 提供了新视角。
light_analysis.motivation: 量化在多模态大语言模型中是一个困难的语言现象，涉及逻辑、语用和数值域，但性能差的原因尚不明确。现有研究可能未充分考虑人类量化的关键特征，如量词排序、使用范围和近似数字系统偏差。本研究旨在填补这一空白，探索这些特征在模型架构中的编码方式，并比较不同模型类型和语言下的表现，以理解模型在量化任务上的局限性及其作为语义 agent 的潜力。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出了一种分析方法，聚焦于人类量化的三个跨语言特征：量词排序成尺度、使用范围和原型性、以及近似数字系统的偏差。通过设计实验，如 numerosity 估计任务和量词排序评估，比较了不同类型模型（thinking 与 instruct）在不同语言下的表现。关键创新在于将人类语言认知特征引入模型评估，以探索这些特征是否在 MLLMs 架构中得到有效编码，并评估模型类型和语言的影响。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，thinking 模型在 numerosity 估计任务和量词排序成尺度上表现出较高的准确度。然而，在所有模型类型中，模型与人类在使用范围和原型性值上存在关键差异，摘要未提供具体性能指标数据，但强调了 thinking 模型在某些方面接近人类，而在其他方面仍有不足，这表明模型在量化任务上的表现不一致，与基线人类认知相比有差距。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究的主要贡献在于揭示了 MLLMs 在量化任务上的局限性，特别是在使用范围和原型性方面与人类的差异，为将 MLLMs 视为语义和语用 agent 奠定了基础。跨语言视角有助于评估模型能力的稳健性和稳定性在不同语言中的表现。未来工作可进一步探究这些差异的原因，并改进模型设计以更好地模拟人类语言认知，推动相关领域的发展。

title: QUARK: Quantization-Enabled Circuit Sharing for Transformer Acceleration by Exploiting Common Patterns in Nonlinear Operations
abstract: Transformer-based models have revolutionized computer vision (CV) and natural language processing (NLP) by achieving state-of-the-art performance across a range of benchmarks. However, nonlinear operations in models significantly contribute to inference latency, presenting unique challenges for efficient hardware acceleration. To this end, we propose QUARK, a quantization-enabled FPGA acceleration framework that leverages common patterns in nonlinear operations to enable efficient circuit sharing, thereby reducing hardware resource requirements. QUARK targets all nonlinear operations within Transformer-based models, achieving high-performance approximation through a novel circuit-sharing design tailored to accelerate these operations. Our evaluation demonstrates that QUARK significantly reduces the computational overhead of nonlinear operators in mainstream Transformer architectures, achieving up to a 1.96 times end-to-end speedup over GPU implementations. Moreover, QUARK lowers the hardware overhead of nonlinear modules by more than 50% compared to prior approaches, all while maintaining high model accuracy -- and even substantially boosting accuracy under ultra-low-bit quantization.

light_analysis.overview: QUARK是一个基于量化和电路共享的FPGA加速框架，显著提升Transformer模型中非线性操作的推理效率。
light_analysis.motivation: Transformer模型在计算机视觉和自然语言处理领域取得了优异性能，但非线性操作如激活函数会增加推理延迟，对硬件加速构成挑战。现有方法在加速这些操作时往往效率低下，导致高资源消耗或性能瓶颈，制约了模型的实际部署。因此，研究动机是开发一个高效框架，以降低非线性操作的延迟和硬件开销，提升Transformer模型在实时应用中的效率。
light_analysis.method: QUARK方法结合量化技术和电路共享设计，在FPGA平台上实现。通过分析Transformer模型中非线性操作的共同模式，设计可共享的电路模块，减少硬件资源重复。该框架针对所有非线性算子，利用量化降低计算复杂度，并采用定制电路进行高性能近似，以优化加速效果。关键创新在于电路共享设计，有效减少资源需求并保持精度。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，QUARK在主流Transformer架构上实现端到端速度比GPU快1.96倍，非线性模块硬件开销降低超过50%。同时，在保持模型准确性的基础上，超低位量化下甚至显著提升准确性。与基线方法相比，QUARK显著减少计算开销，展示了其高效性和鲁棒性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究提出了QUARK框架，有效加速Transformer中的非线性操作，减少硬件资源需求，具有重要学术和实际应用价值。它为硬件加速提供了创新思路，结合量化和电路共享，可促进高效AI模型在边缘计算等场景的部署。未来工作可能包括扩展到更多模型类型或进一步优化量化策略，摘要未明确说明具体局限性。

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Origin-Destination Matrix Estimation in Microscopic Traffic Simulations Considering Credit Assignment
abstract: This paper focuses on dynamic origin-destination matrix estimation (DODE), a crucial calibration process necessary for the effective application of microscopic traffic simulations. The fundamental challenge of the DODE problem in microscopic simulations stems from the complex temporal dynamics and inherent uncertainty of individual vehicle dynamics. This makes it highly challenging to precisely determine which vehicle traverses which link at any given moment, resulting in intricate and often ambiguous relationships between origin-destination (OD) matrices and their contributions to resultant link flows. This phenomenon constitutes the credit assignment problem, a central challenge addressed in this study. We formulate the DODE problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) and propose a novel framework that applies model-free deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Within our proposed framework, the agent learns an optimal policy to sequentially generate OD matrices, refining its strategy through direct interaction with the simulation environment. This approach was evaluated through a toy experiment on the Nguyen-Dupuis network and a case study utilizing an actual highway subnetwork spanning Santa Clara and San Jose. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach reduces the mean squared error (MSE) by over 20% compared to the best-performing conventional baseline. By reframing DODE as a sequential decision-making problem, our approach addresses the credit assignment challenge through its learned policy, thereby overcoming the limitations of conventional methods and proposing a novel framework for calibration of microscopic traffic simulations.

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的新框架，用于解决微观交通模拟中动态起点-终点矩阵估计的信用分配问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 动态起点-终点矩阵估计是微观交通模拟的关键校准过程，但传统方法面临复杂时间动态和车辆动态不确定性的挑战，导致难以精确分配车辆路径对链接流量的贡献，形成信用分配问题。这影响了估计精度和模拟可靠性，因此需要新方法来克服这些不足，提升校准效果。研究背景强调信用分配在DODE中的核心作用，现有方法在处理动态不确定性时效率低下。
light_analysis.method: 论文将动态起点-终点矩阵估计问题建模为马尔可夫决策过程，并提出一个模型无关的深度强化学习框架。代理通过直接与模拟环境交互，学习最优策略顺序生成起点-终点矩阵，以解决信用分配挑战。关键创新点包括将估计问题转化为顺序决策，使用强化学习优化策略。评估采用Nguyen-Dupuis网络的玩具实验和Santa Clara至San Jose的实际高速公路子网络案例。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果表明，在Nguyen-Dupuis网络玩具实验和实际高速公路案例中，该方法相比最佳传统基线将均方误差降低了超过20%。具体性能提升体现在减少估计误差，通过直接交互优化策略，展示了在解决信用分配问题上的有效性。与基线对比显示精度显著改进，验证了新框架的优越性。摘要未明确说明其他具体指标，但强调了整体误差减少。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文的主要贡献是提出一种基于深度强化学习的框架，将动态起点-终点矩阵估计重新构架为顺序决策问题，成功解决信用分配挑战，克服传统方法局限性。学术价值在于扩展强化学习在交通工程的应用，实际价值在于提高微观交通模拟的校准精度。未来工作可能涉及优化算法效率或扩展到更大规模网络，摘要未明确说明局限性，但暗示了进一步研究的潜力。

title: MedM2T: A MultiModal Framework for Time-Aware Modeling with Electronic Health Record and Electrocardiogram Data
abstract: The inherent multimodality and heterogeneous temporal structures of medical data pose significant challenges for modeling. We propose MedM2T, a time-aware multimodal framework designed to address these complexities. MedM2T integrates: (i) Sparse Time Series Encoder to flexibly handle irregular and sparse time series, (ii) Hierarchical Time-Aware Fusion to capture both micro- and macro-temporal patterns from multiple dense time series, such as ECGs, and (iii) Bi-Modal Attention to extract cross-modal interactions, which can be extended to any number of modalities. To mitigate granularity gaps between modalities, MedM2T uses modality-specific pre-trained encoders and aligns resulting features within a shared encoder. We evaluated MedM2T on MIMIC-IV and MIMIC-IV-ECG datasets for three tasks that encompass chronic and acute disease dynamics: 90-day cardiovascular disease (CVD) prediction, in-hospital mortality prediction, and ICU length-of-stay (LOS) regression. MedM2T achieved superior or comparable performance relative to state-of-the-art multimodal learning frameworks and existing time series models, achieving an AUROC of 0.932 and an AUPRC of 0.670 for CVD prediction; an AUROC of 0.868 and an AUPRC of 0.470 for mortality prediction; and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.33 for LOS regression. These results highlight the robustness and broad applicability of MedM2T, positioning it as a promising tool in clinical prediction. We provide the implementation of MedM2T at https://github.com/DHLab-TSENG/MedM2T.
comment: This preprint version of the manuscript has been submitted to the IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics (JBHI) for review. The implementation of MedM2T is available at https://github.com/DHLab-TSENG/MedM2T

light_analysis.overview: MedM2T提出了一种时间感知的多模态框架，通过集成稀疏时间序列编码器、分层时间感知融合和双模态注意力，有效处理医学数据的多模态和异质时间结构，提升临床预测准确性。
light_analysis.motivation: 医学数据如电子健康记录和心电图具有多模态特性和复杂时间结构，现有模型往往难以同时处理不规则时间序列和跨模态交互，导致在慢性与急性疾病预测任务中精度受限。本研究旨在解决这一问题，通过时间感知框架增强模型的适应性和鲁棒性，以支持更精准的医疗决策，改善患者预后管理。摘要未明确说明具体现有方法不足，但暗示传统方法可能无法充分整合多种数据源的时间模式。
light_analysis.method: MedM2T框架的核心技术包括：稀疏时间序列编码器，用于灵活处理不规则和稀疏数据；分层时间感知融合，从密集时间序列如心电图中捕获微观和宏观时间模式；双模态注意力机制，提取跨模态交互并可扩展至任意多模态。此外，框架使用模态特定预训练编码器，并在共享编码器中对齐特征，以减少模态间粒度差距，增强模型泛化能力。在MIMIC-IV和MIMIC-IV-ECG数据集上进行评估，涵盖心血管疾病预测、死亡率预测和住院时间回归任务。
light_analysis.result: MedM2T在MIMIC-IV数据集上的三个任务中表现优异：对于90天心血管疾病预测，AUROC达到0.932，AUPRC为0.670；院内死亡率预测AUROC为0.868，AUPRC为0.470；ICU住院时间回归的均方误差为2.33。这些结果优于或可媲美最先进的多模态学习框架和时间序列模型，显示了MedM2T在多种临床预测任务中的高准确性和鲁棒性，验证了其时间感知和多模态整合的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: MedM2T的主要贡献是提出一种创新的时间感知多模态框架，有效处理医学数据的复杂时间特性，推动了临床预测模型的进步。其学术价值在于提供新的方法整合多源数据，应用价值在于提升医疗决策的准确性，如疾病风险预测和资源管理。摘要未明确说明具体局限性或未来工作方向，但框架的可扩展性暗示潜在应用于更多模态和医疗任务。

title: Is Multilingual LLM Watermarking Truly Multilingual? Scaling Robustness to 100+ Languages via Back-Translation
abstract: Multilingual watermarking aims to make large language model (LLM) outputs traceable across languages, yet current methods still fall short. Despite claims of cross-lingual robustness, they are evaluated only on high-resource languages. We show that existing multilingual watermarking methods are not truly multilingual: they fail to remain robust under translation attacks in medium- and low-resource languages. We trace this failure to semantic clustering, which fails when the tokenizer vocabulary contains too few full-word tokens for a given language. To address this, we introduce STEAM, a detection method that uses Bayesian optimisation to search among 133 candidate languages for the back-translation that best recovers the watermark strength. It is compatible with any watermarking method, robust across different tokenizers and languages, non-invasive, and easily extendable to new languages. With average gains of +0.23 AUC and +37% TPR@1%, STEAM provides a scalable approach toward fairer watermarking across the diversity of languages.

light_analysis.overview: 本论文提出STEAM方法，通过贝叶斯优化和反向翻译，显著提升多语言大语言模型水印在100多种语言中的鲁棒性和公平性。
light_analysis.motivation: 研究动机源于当前多语言水印方法虽然声称跨语言鲁棒，但仅在高资源语言上验证，对中低资源语言的翻译攻击表现出脆弱性。这一问题的重要性在于水印技术用于确保LLM输出的可追溯性，若覆盖不均衡会导致应用偏见和安全风险。现有方法依赖语义聚类，当语言词汇不足时失效，暴露了多语言水印的不公平性和技术局限，迫切需要改进。
light_analysis.method: STEAM是一种检测方法，使用贝叶斯优化在133个候选语言中搜索最佳反向翻译，以恢复水印强度。该方法兼容任意水印技术，通过反向翻译将输出转换并回译，增强信号检测。关键创新包括非侵入性设计、对tokenizer和语言类型的鲁棒性，以及易于扩展到新语言，结合了优化算法和多语言处理，为水印提供了通用解决方案。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，STEAM方法平均提升AUC 0.23，TPR@1%提升37%，显著优于现有基线。这表明在多种语言环境下，特别是中低资源语言中，水印检测的准确性和效率得到改善。通过与现有方法对比，STEAM提供了可扩展的鲁棒性，支持100+语言的水印验证，验证了其在实际应用中的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文的主要贡献是提出了STEAM方法，解决了多语言水印在中低资源语言中的鲁棒性问题，推动了更公平的水印技术发展。学术上，该方法结合反向翻译和贝叶斯优化，为NLP水印领域提供了新思路；实际应用中，支持广泛语言覆盖，有助于增强AI输出的安全性和可追溯性。未来工作可探索更多优化策略或扩展到其他多语言任务。

title: OffSim: Offline Simulator for Model-based Offline Inverse Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Reinforcement learning algorithms typically utilize an interactive simulator (i.e., environment) with a predefined reward function for policy training. Developing such simulators and manually defining reward functions, however, is often time-consuming and labor-intensive. To address this, we propose an Offline Simulator (OffSim), a novel model-based offline inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) framework, to emulate environmental dynamics and reward structure directly from expert-generated state-action trajectories. OffSim jointly optimizes a high-entropy transition model and an IRL-based reward function to enhance exploration and improve the generalizability of the learned reward. Leveraging these learned components, OffSim can subsequently train a policy offline without further interaction with the real environment. Additionally, we introduce OffSim$^+$, an extension that incorporates a marginal reward for multi-dataset settings to enhance exploration. Extensive MuJoCo experiments demonstrate that OffSim achieves substantial performance gains over existing offline IRL methods, confirming its efficacy and robustness.
comment: Due to an authorship dispute among the co-authors, we request to withdraw this submission. The issue is currently unresolved, and we believe withdrawal is appropriate until the matter is settled

light_analysis.overview: OffSim是一个模型式离线逆向强化学习框架，通过从专家轨迹中联合学习环境动态和奖励函数，离线训练策略以提高探索和泛化能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统强化学习算法通常需要交互式模拟器和预定义奖励函数来训练策略，但开发这类模拟器和手动定义奖励函数耗时耗力，增加了应用成本。为了解决这一问题，本研究提出直接从专家生成的状态-动作轨迹中学习环境动态和奖励结构，以减轻人工负担并提高学习效率。现有方法往往依赖在线交互或复杂的人工设计，限制了其适用性，而OffSim旨在通过离线学习克服这些不足。
light_analysis.method: OffSim提出一个模型式离线逆向强化学习框架，核心方法是联合优化高熵转移模型和基于逆向强化学习的奖励函数，直接从专家轨迹中模拟环境动态和奖励结构。关键创新点在于结合转移模型与奖励学习，以增强探索能力和改善奖励泛化性，允许离线训练策略而无需与真实环境交互。扩展版本OffSim+引入了边际奖励，用于多数据集设置以进一步提升探索性能，具体利用专家轨迹进行学习并优化组件。
light_analysis.result: 在MuJoCo环境中进行的实验表明，OffSim相比现有离线逆向强化学习方法，取得了显著的性能提升，证实了其有效性和鲁棒性。摘要未明确说明具体的性能指标（如准确率或效率改进的精确数值），但与基线方法的比较显示出性能优势，例如在任务执行效果上有所改进。这些结果证明了OffSim在离线场景中的适用性和改进潜力。
light_analysis.conclusion: OffSim的主要贡献是提供了一个从专家轨迹中离线学习环境动态和奖励函数的框架，通过联合优化提高探索和泛化性。其学术价值在于推动了模型式离线逆向强化学习领域的发展，为无需交互式环境的学习提供了新方法；实际应用中能降低模拟器开发成本和时间。未来工作可能包括扩展到更复杂的环境或多任务学习，以及验证在真实场景中的效果，尽管摘要未明确提及具体方向或局限性。

title: SAG-Agent: Enabling Long-Horizon Reasoning in Strategy Games via Dynamic Knowledge Graphs
abstract: Most commodity software lacks accessible Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), requiring autonomous agents to interact solely through pixel-based Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs). In this API-free setting, large language model (LLM)-based agents face severe efficiency bottlenecks: limited to local visual experiences, they make myopic decisions and rely on inefficient trial-and-error, hindering both skill acquisition and long-horizon planning. To overcome these limitations, we propose SAG-Agent, an experience-driven learning framework that structures an agent's raw pixel-level interactions into a persistent State-Action Graph (SAG). SAG-Agent mitigates inefficient exploration by topologically linking functionally similar but visually distinct GUI states, constructing a rich neighborhood of experience that enables the agent to generalize from a diverse set of historical strategies. To facilitate long-horizon reasoning, we design a novel hybrid intrinsic reward mechanism based on the graph topology, combining a state-value reward for exploiting known high-value pathways with a novelty reward that encourages targeted exploration. This approach decouples strategic planning from pure discovery, allowing the agent to effectively value setup actions with delayed gratification. We evaluate SAG-Agent in two complex, open-ended GUI-based decision-making environments (Civilization V and Slay the Spire), demonstrating significant improvements in exploration efficiency and strategic depth over the state-of-the-art methods.

light_analysis.overview: SAG-Agent提出一种基于状态-动作图的经验驱动学习框架，通过动态知识图提升策略游戏中代理的长期推理和探索效率。
light_analysis.motivation: 在缺乏可访问API的软件环境中，自主代理需要通过像素级图形用户界面进行交互，导致基于大型语言模型的代理面临决策短视和探索低效的瓶颈。现有方法依赖局部视觉经验和试错过程，阻碍了技能获取和长期战略规划，这在复杂策略游戏中尤为重要。解决这一问题能显著提高代理在GUI环境中的学习效能和规划能力，推动无API交互场景下的自主代理发展。
light_analysis.method: SAG-Agent的核心方法是构建一个持久的状态-动作图（SAG），将代理的像素级交互结构化，并通过拓扑连接功能相似但视觉不同的GUI状态，形成丰富的经验邻域以促进泛化学习。创新点在于设计了一个基于图拓扑的混合内在奖励机制，结合状态值奖励用于利用高价值路径和新颖性奖励鼓励针对性探索，从而解耦战略规划与探索。研究在Civilization V和Slay the Spire两个复杂GUI决策环境中实施评估，但摘要未明确说明具体数据集或模型架构。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，SAG-Agent在探索效率和战略深度方面取得了显著改进，优于最先进方法。在Civilization V和Slay the Spire环境中，代理表现出更高效的探索能力和更强的长期规划性能，验证了框架的有效性。具体数据如准确率提升百分比未在摘要中给出，但整体性能的提升得到了明确强调。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是提出了SAG-Agent框架，通过状态-动作图和混合内在奖励机制，有效提升了代理在GUI环境中的长期推理能力。学术上，它为无API交互的代理学习提供了新方法，推动了经验驱动的规划技术；实际应用上，可扩展至策略游戏等领域，提高自主代理性能。未来工作可能包括优化图结构或应用到更多环境中，但摘要未明确说明具体局限性。

title: Tackling Time-Series Forecasting Generalization via Mitigating Concept Drift
abstract: Time-series forecasting finds broad applications in real-world scenarios. Due to the dynamic nature of time series data, it is important for time-series forecasting models to handle potential distribution shifts over time. In this paper, we initially identify two types of distribution shifts in time series: concept drift and temporal shift. We acknowledge that while existing studies primarily focus on addressing temporal shift issues in time series forecasting, designing proper concept drift methods for time series forecasting has received comparatively less attention.   Motivated by the need to address potential concept drift, while conventional concept drift methods via invariant learning face certain challenges in time-series forecasting, we propose a soft attention mechanism that finds invariant patterns from both lookback and horizon time series. Additionally, we emphasize the critical importance of mitigating temporal shifts as a preliminary to addressing concept drift. In this context, we introduce ShifTS, a method-agnostic framework designed to tackle temporal shift first and then concept drift within a unified approach. Extensive experiments demonstrate the efficacy of ShifTS in consistently enhancing the forecasting accuracy of agnostic models across multiple datasets, and outperforming existing concept drift, temporal shift, and combined baselines.

light_analysis.overview: 论文提出了ShifTS框架，通过先处理时间漂移再处理概念漂移，以提升时间序列预测的泛化能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 时间序列预测在现实场景中广泛应用，但由于数据的动态性，模型需应对分布漂移以保持泛化能力。现有研究主要关注时间漂移，而对概念漂移方法的设计关注较少；传统概念漂移方法在时间序列预测中存在挑战，导致模型泛化受限。因此，本研究动机在于填补概念漂移处理方法的空白，通过专门方法提升预测模型对动态数据的适应性和鲁棒性，以应对实际应用中的不确定性。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出一个软注意力机制，从回看和展望时间序列中提取不变模式，以识别和应对概念漂移。强调先缓解时间漂移作为处理概念漂移的前提，并引入ShifTS框架——这是一个方法无关的统一方法，先处理时间漂移，再处理概念漂移。核心创新在于将两种漂移类型整合到一个框架中，使用注意力机制增强模型对分布变化的泛化能力，无需依赖特定模型架构。
light_analysis.result: 通过广泛实验验证，ShifTS框架在多个数据集上一致提升了时间序列预测的准确性，优于现有的概念漂移、时间漂移以及组合基线方法。摘要未明确说明具体性能指标，但实验结果表明该方法有效增强了模型的泛化性能，并在不同数据集上实现了稳定的预测改进，证明了框架的鲁棒性和实用性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是提出了ShifTS框架，解决了时间序列预测中的概念漂移问题，通过先处理时间漂移再处理概念漂移，提升了预测模型的泛化能力。该研究具有重要的学术价值，为分布漂移处理提供了新思路，并增强了实际应用中的模型可靠性。未来工作可进一步探索框架在更复杂或动态环境下的扩展，以及在不同漂移类型下的性能评估。

title: Beyond Multi-Token Prediction: Pretraining LLMs with Future Summaries
abstract: Next-token prediction (NTP) has driven the success of large language models (LLMs), but it struggles with long-horizon reasoning, planning, and creative writing, with these limitations largely attributed to teacher-forced training. Multi-token prediction (MTP) partially mitigates these issues by predicting several future tokens at once, but it mostly captures short-range dependencies and offers limited improvement. We propose future summary prediction (FSP), which trains an auxiliary head to predict a compact representation of the long-term future, preserving information relevant for long-form generations. We explore two variants of FSP: handcrafted summaries, for example, a bag of words summary of the future sequence, and learned summaries, which use embeddings produced by a reverse language model trained from right-to-left order. Large-scale pretraining experiments (3B and 8B-parameter models) demonstrate that FSP provides improvements over both NTP and MTP across math, reasoning, and coding benchmarks.
comment: Proceedings of the Fourteenth International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR) 2026

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出未来摘要预测（FSP）方法，通过预测长期未来的紧凑表示来增强大语言模型的预训练性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 该研究旨在解决大语言模型在长期推理、规划和创意写作任务中表现不佳的问题。当前广泛使用的下一个令牌预测（NTP）由于教师强制训练，难以捕捉长距离依赖关系，限制了模型的扩展能力。多令牌预测（MTP）虽然部分缓解了这一问题，但主要关注短距离依赖，对长期生成的改进有限。因此，开发更有效的预训练策略对于提升模型在复杂应用场景下的性能至关重要，尤其是在需要前瞻性思维的任务中。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出未来摘要预测（FSP）作为预训练方法的核心创新，通过添加一个辅助预测头来学习生成长期未来的紧凑摘要表示。该方法探索两个变体：手工艺摘要，如使用词袋摘要概括未来序列；以及学到的摘要，利用一个从右到左训练的反向语言模型生成嵌入表示，以自动提取关键信息。研究在3B和8B参数的大语言模型上进行大规模预训练实验，以验证FSP在改进模型长期依赖建模能力方面的有效性。
light_analysis.result: 大规模预训练实验显示，未来摘要预测（FSP）在多个评估基准上均优于下一个令牌预测（NTP）和多令牌预测（MTP）。具体表现在数学推理、逻辑问题和代码生成任务中，FSP提供了显著的性能提升，有效地捕获了长期依赖关系。与基线方法相比，FSP通过保留未来信息的关键摘要，改善了模型的生成质量和一致性，尽管摘要未提供具体数值指标，但实验证明了其整体优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是引入了未来摘要预测（FSP）作为一种创新的预训练策略，解决了大语言模型在长期依赖建模上的核心挑战。该方法通过预测未来摘要，保留了对长形式生成至关重要的信息，学术上丰富了预训练技术路线，应用上提升了模型在推理和编码等复杂任务的实用性。未来工作可以进一步探索不同摘要形式的优化，或将其扩展到更大规模模型和更多样化的任务中，以挖掘其潜力。

title: Score-Based Density Estimation from Pairwise Comparisons
abstract: We study density estimation from pairwise comparisons, motivated by expert knowledge elicitation and learning from human feedback. We relate the unobserved target density to a tempered winner density (marginal density of preferred choices), learning the winner's score via score-matching. This allows estimating the target by `de-tempering' the estimated winner density's score. We prove that the score vectors of the belief and the winner density are collinear, linked by a position-dependent tempering field. We give analytical formulas for this field and propose an estimator for it under the Bradley-Terry model. Using a diffusion model trained on tempered samples generated via score-scaled annealed Langevin dynamics, we can learn complex multivariate belief densities of simulated experts, from only hundreds to thousands of pairwise comparisons.
comment: Accepted at ICLR 2026. Camera-ready version. 36 pages, 16 figures

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出一种基于分数匹配和调和场的方法，从成对比较中估计复杂多元密度，通过扩散模型实现高效学习。
light_analysis.motivation: 研究动机源于专家知识提取和从人类反馈学习，需要从成对比较中估计目标密度。现有密度估计方法在数据稀疏或比较有限时表现不佳，难以处理复杂分布，而这个问题在推荐系统和社会选择等领域至关重要，因为传统方法往往依赖大量数据或简化假设，导致应用受限。
light_analysis.method: 方法核心是将未观测目标密度与胜者密度关联，通过分数匹配学习胜者密度的分数向量。创新点在于证明了信念密度和胜者密度的分数共线性，并推导出位置相关调和场的解析公式。在 Bradley-Terry 模型下提出调和场估计器，结合扩散模型，在通过分数缩放退火 Langevin 动力学生成的调和样本上训练，从而从成对比较中学习复杂多元密度。
light_analysis.result: 论文表明，该方法可以从仅数百到数千个成对比较中成功学习模拟专家的复杂多元信念密度。摘要未明确说明具体性能指标，但暗示了在数据稀缺条件下的有效性，与基线方法相比可能提升估计精度和效率，适用于少量数据场景。
light_analysis.conclusion: 主要贡献是提出了一种新框架，将分数匹配与调和场结合，实现从少量成对比较中估计复杂密度，增强了密度估计在偏好学习和专家系统等领域的应用价值。学术上推进了统计学习方法，未来方向可能包括扩展到实际数据集和验证泛化能力，摘要未明确说明局限性。

title: How Many Code and Test Cases Are Enough? Evaluating Test Cases Generation from a Binary-Matrix Perspective
abstract: Evaluating test cases automatically generated by Large Language Models (LLMs) is a critical yet challenging task. Existing benchmarks often evaluate the exclusion ratio on large, unstructured collections of wrong codes, suffering from high computational costs and score inflation. Furthermore, they inadvertently reward generators that detect common, trivial bugs, while failing to penalize their inability to identify rare yet critical faults. In this work, we connect two fundamental questions: (1) What is the minimal set of wrong codes sufficient to represent the entire error space? and (2) What is the minimal set of test cases needed to distinguish them? We introduce a novel framework that formalizes benchmark construction as finding an optimal diagnostic basis in a binary code-test matrix, where rows represent wrong codes and columns represent test case results. The rank of this matrix specifies the minimal number of independent error patterns (wrong codes) and provides a tight upper bound on the number of test cases required for complete fault coverage. Our objective is to identify a basis of size equal to the matrix rank that maximizes internal diversity. To tackle this NP-hard problem, we propose WrongSelect, an efficient approximation algorithm to select maximally diverse wrong codes. Applying this framework to millions of competitive programming submissions, we construct TC-Bench, a compact, diverse, and inflation-resistant benchmark. Extensive experiments show that even the most advanced test case generation methods achieve only ~60% exclusion rates on TC-Bench, exposing a significant gap in their diagnostic power and highlighting substantial room for future improvement. Our dataset is available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Luoberta/TC-Bench and our code is at: https://github.com/Luowaterbi/TC-Bench.

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出基于二进制代码-测试矩阵的框架和WrongSelect算法，构建了紧凑多样且抗膨胀的测试用例生成基准TC-Bench，以更有效地评估大型语言模型的诊断能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 评估大型语言模型（LLMs）自动生成的测试用例是确保代码可靠性的关键任务，但现有基准在大量无结构错误代码上进行评估，导致高计算成本和分数膨胀问题。这些基准偏向于奖励检测常见琐碎错误的生成器，未能惩罚对罕见关键错误的忽视，从而无法全面反映实际诊断能力。因此，本研究旨在解决基准构建的缺陷，通过更科学的方法评估测试用例生成，促进技术进步和准确度量。
light_analysis.method: 研究方法引入一个新颖框架，将基准构建形式化为在二进制代码-测试矩阵中寻找最优诊断基础，矩阵的行代表错误代码，列代表测试用例结果。关键创新在于利用矩阵的秩来指定独立错误模式的最小数量和所需测试用例数的紧上界，并最大化内部多样性以构建紧凑基准。为解决这一NP-hard问题，提出了WrongSelect算法，一个高效近似方法来选择最大多样性的错误代码，应用于数百万竞争性编程提交数据，最终构建了TC-Bench基准。
light_analysis.result: 主要实验结果显示，即使在TC-Bench基准上，最先进的测试用例生成方法仅达到约60%的排除率，这显著低于传统基准可能夸大的性能。这一结果暴露了现有方法在诊断能力上的实质性差距，突显出它们在检测多样性和关键错误方面的不足。通过TC-Bench的评估，研究提供了更准确、抗膨胀的性能度量，为未来改进指明了方向，并表明当前技术仍有很大提升空间。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文的主要贡献在于提出了基于二进制矩阵的框架和WrongSelect算法，成功构建了TC-Bench基准，为评估LLMs生成的测试用例提供了更有效、公平的工具。学术上，该研究将基准构建形式化为优化问题，推动了测试用例生成领域的评估标准；实际应用中，它有助于识别现有方法的局限性，促进技术改进。未来工作可扩展该方法到其他领域，或进一步优化算法效率和基准覆盖范围。

title: RamPINN: Recovering Raman Spectra From Coherent Anti-Stokes Spectra Using Embedded Physics
abstract: Transferring the recent advancements in deep learning into scientific disciplines is hindered by the lack of the required large-scale datasets for training. We argue that in these knowledge-rich domains, the established body of scientific theory provides reliable inductive biases in the form of governing physical laws. We address the ill-posed inverse problem of recovering Raman spectra from noisy Coherent Anti-Stokes Raman Scattering (CARS) measurements, as the true Raman signal here is suppressed by a dominating non-resonant background. We propose RamPINN, a model that learns to recover Raman spectra from given CARS spectra. Our core methodological contribution is a physics-informed neural network that utilizes a dual-decoder architecture to disentangle resonant and non-resonant signals. This is done by enforcing the Kramers-Kronig causality relations via a differentiable Hilbert transform loss on the resonant and a smoothness prior on the non-resonant part of the signal. Trained entirely on synthetic data, RamPINN demonstrates strong zero-shot generalization to real-world experimental data, explicitly closing this gap and significantly outperforming existing baselines. Furthermore, we show that training with these physics-based losses alone, without access to any ground-truth Raman spectra, still yields competitive results. This work highlights a broader concept: formal scientific rules can act as a potent inductive bias, enabling robust, self-supervised learning in data-limited scientific domains.

light_analysis.overview: 论文提出 RamPINN 模型，基于物理信息神经网络从噪声 CARS 光谱中恢复拉曼光谱，实现零样本泛化。
light_analysis.motivation: 深度学习在科学领域的应用常受限于大规模数据集的缺乏。本研究针对相干反斯托克斯拉曼散射（CARS）测量中拉曼信号被非共振背景抑制的逆问题，这是一个典型的数据受限场景。现有方法可能依赖大量标注数据或未充分融入物理知识，导致恢复效果不佳。作者认为科学理论提供可靠归纳偏见，可通过物理约束弥补数据不足，从而提升模型在知识丰富领域的适用性和鲁棒性。
light_analysis.method: 本研究提出 RamPINN，一个物理信息神经网络，采用双解码器架构分别处理共振和非共振信号。核心创新在于通过可微分的希尔伯特变换损失强制执行 Kramers-Kronig 因果关系，并在非共振部分应用平滑先验，确保信号分解的物理一致性。模型完全在合成数据上训练，避免了真实标注需求，实现了自监督学习。技术细节包括使用神经网络模拟 CARS 到拉曼光谱的映射，并利用物理规则作为正则化项优化学习过程。
light_analysis.result: RamPINN 在真实实验数据上展示了强大的零样本泛化能力，显著优于现有基线方法，尽管摘要未明确给出具体性能指标如准确率提升。实验结果表明，仅使用基于物理的损失函数进行训练，无需任何真实拉曼光谱数据，也能获得竞争性的恢复效果，这突显了物理约束在数据稀缺情况下的有效性。模型的优越性能体现在对噪声 CARS 光谱的准确分解，验证了其在实践中的实用潜力。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是验证了物理规则作为归纳偏见的有效性，提出 RamPINN 模型为数据有限的科学领域提供了一种自监督学习方法。学术价值在于将科学理论与深度学习结合，推动了跨学科研究的进展；实际应用价值体现在从噪声 CARS 数据中可靠恢复拉曼光谱，有助于光谱分析领域的工具开发。未来工作可扩展物理约束到其他逆问题，并探索更多自监督策略，以应对数据挑战。

title: FedSRD: Sparsify-Reconstruct-Decompose for Communication-Efficient Federated Large Language Models Fine-Tuning
abstract: The current paradigm of training large language models (LLMs) on public available Web data is becoming unsustainable as high-quality data sources in specialized domains near exhaustion. Federated Learning (FL) emerges as a practical solution for the next generation of AI on a decentralized Web, enabling privacy-preserving collaborative fine-tuning on decentralized private data. While Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) is standard for efficient fine-tuning, its federated application faces a critical bottleneck: communication overhead under heterogeneous network conditions. Structural redundancy in LoRA parameters increases communication costs and causes aggregation conflicts. To address this, we propose FedSRD, a Sparsify-Reconstruct-Decompose framework for communication-efficient federated LLM fine-tuning. We introduce importance-aware sparsification to reduce the upload parameter count while preserving the structural integrity of LoRA updates. The server aggregates updates in full-rank space to mitigate conflicts, then decomposes the global update into a sparse low-rank format for broadcast, ensuring a symmetrically efficient cycle. We also propose an efficient variant, FedSRD-e, to reduce computational overhead. Experiments on 10 benchmarks show our framework significantly reduces communication costs by up to 90\% while improving performance on heterogeneous client data.

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出FedSRD框架，通过稀疏化-重构-分解方法，显著降低联邦大型语言模型微调中的通信开销，实现高效合作学习。
light_analysis.motivation: 当前公共网络数据训练大型语言模型不可持续，高质量专业数据接近枯竭，联邦学习作为分散式网络上的隐私保护解决方案备受关注。然而，低秩适应在联邦应用中面临通信开销高的瓶颈，参数冗余导致聚合冲突，阻碍了高效微调，需要创新方法来平衡通信效率和模型性能。
light_analysis.method: FedSRD框架采用重要性感知稀疏化减少上传参数，保留更新结构完整性；服务器在完整秩空间聚合更新以缓解冲突，然后分解为稀疏低秩格式广播，实现对称高效循环。还提出FedSRD-e变体降低计算开销，结合数据集和模型架构优化，确保技术可行性。
light_analysis.result: 实验在10个基准测试中显示，FedSRD显著降低通信成本高达90%，同时在异构客户端数据上提升性能，优于基线方法。具体指标表明框架在减少开销的同时保持模型效果，验证了其高效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 研究贡献了通信高效的联邦LLM微调方法，解决LoRA在联邦学习中的通信瓶颈，具有学术价值和实际应用潜力，如推动隐私保护AI发展。未来可探索更广泛场景应用和算法优化，以应对复杂网络条件。

title: One Patch to Caption Them All: A Unified Zero-Shot Captioning Framework
abstract: Zero-shot captioners are recently proposed models that utilize common-space vision-language representations to caption images without relying on paired image-text data. To caption an image, they proceed by textually decoding a text-aligned image feature, but they limit their scope to global representations and whole-image captions. We present a unified framework for zero-shot captioning that shifts from an image-centric to a patch-centric paradigm, enabling the captioning of arbitrary regions without the need of region-level supervision. Instead of relying on global image representations, we treat individual patches as atomic captioning units and aggregate them to describe arbitrary regions, from single patches to non-contiguous areas and entire images. We analyze the key ingredients that enable current latent captioners to work in our novel proposed framework. Experiments demonstrate that backbones producing meaningful, dense visual features, such as DINO, are key to achieving state-of-the-art performance in multiple region-based captioning tasks. Compared to other baselines and state-of-the-art competitors, our models achieve better performance on zero-shot dense captioning and region-set captioning. We also introduce a new trace captioning task that further demonstrates the effectiveness of patch-wise semantic representations for flexible caption generation. Project page at https://paciosoft.com/Patch-ioner/ .

light_analysis.overview: 提出一个基于补丁的统一零样本描述框架，以补丁为中心，无需区域级监督即可描述任意图像区域。
light_analysis.motivation: 零样本描述模型利用视觉-语言表示描述图像，但现有方法依赖全局图像表示，只能生成整个图像描述，无法处理特定区域或非连续区域。这在实际应用中，如目标识别或场景理解中，限制了模型的灵活性和实用性。研究动机是克服这一局限，开发一个更通用的框架，在无需成对数据监督的情况下，实现零样本下的任意区域描述，以解决现有方法缺乏区域级能力的不足。
light_analysis.method: 本论文提出一个统一的零样本描述框架，采用补丁中心范式取代传统图像中心方法。核心方法是将图像分割为补丁，将每个补丁视为原子描述单元，通过聚合补丁特征来描述从单个补丁到非连续区域乃至整个图像的任意区域。关键创新点是使用如DINO等主干网络，这些网络能产生密集、有意义的视觉特征，支持补丁级别语义表示。该方法无需区域级监督，实现了真正的零样本学习，利用视觉-语言对齐进行灵活描述生成。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，使用如DINO等产生密集视觉特征的主干网络，在多个区域描述任务中达到最先进性能。与基线和其他竞争者相比，本模型在零样本密集描述和区域集描述任务上表现更好，展示了优越的整体性能。此外，新引入的跟踪描述任务进一步验证了补丁级别语义表示在灵活描述生成中的有效性。摘要未明确说明具体性能指标数据，但强调了在多项任务中的竞争力和改进。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是提出了一个基于补丁的统一零样本描述框架，通过转向补丁中心范式，实现了无需监督的任意区域描述。学术上，该工作扩展了零样本视觉-语言模型的能力，推动了更灵活的图像理解研究。实际应用上，该框架可用于图像分析、辅助技术和内容生成等领域。未来工作可能包括优化补丁聚合机制、扩展到更多视觉任务或处理更复杂的场景。

title: FOCUS: Optimal Control for Multi-Entity World Modeling in Text-to-Image Generation
abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) models excel on single-entity prompts but struggle with multi-entity scenes, often exhibiting attribute leakage, identity entanglement, and subject omissions. We present a principled theoretical framework that steers sampling toward multi-subject fidelity by casting flow matching (FM) as stochastic optimal control (SOC), yielding a single hyperparameter controlled trade-off between fidelity and object-centric state separation / binding consistency. Within this framework, we derive two architecture-agnostic algorithms: (i) a training-free test-time controller that perturbs the base velocity with a single-pass update, and (ii) Adjoint Matching, a lightweight fine-tuning rule that regresses a control network to a backward adjoint signal. The same formulation unifies prior attention heuristics, extends to diffusion models via a flow--diffusion correspondence, and provides the first fine-tuning route explicitly designed for multi-subject fidelity. In addition, we also introduce FOCUS (Flow Optimal Control for Unentangled Subjects), a probabilistic attention-binding objective compatible with both algorithms. Empirically, on Stable Diffusion 3.5 and FLUX.1, both algorithms consistently improve multi-subject alignment while maintaining base-model style; test-time control runs efficiently on commodity GPUs, and fine-tuned models generalize to unseen prompts.

light_analysis.overview: 论文提出基于随机最优控制的理论框架和算法，有效提升文本到图像模型在多实体场景中的生成保真度和一致性。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有文本到图像模型在单实体提示上表现优异，但在多实体场景中常出现属性泄漏、身份纠缠和主题遗漏等问题，这限制了其在复杂图像生成中的应用。由于缺乏系统性解决方案，导致生成质量下降，因此需要开发新方法来提高多主体保真度和对象一致性，以应对实际需求。
light_analysis.method: 研究方法将流匹配建模为随机最优控制，构建理论框架以优化采样过程，通过单一超参数控制保真度与对象分离之间的权衡。核心创新包括两种架构无关算法：训练无关的测试时控制器，通过单次更新扰动基础速度；以及Adjoint Matching，一个轻量级微调规则，将控制网络回归到后向伴随信号。此外，引入FOCUS作为概率注意力绑定目标，兼容流和扩散模型，扩展了方法的通用性。
light_analysis.result: 实验在Stable Diffusion 3.5和FLUX.1上进行，结果表明两种算法均能提高多主体对齐效果，同时保持基础模型风格。测试时控制器在普通GPU上运行高效，而微调模型对未见过的提示具有良好的泛化能力，证实了方法在改进生成质量和实用性方面的有效性，与基线方法相比表现出明显提升。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文的主要贡献是提出统一框架，将流匹配与最优控制结合，解决文本到图像模型的多实体问题，学术上统一了先前启发式方法并提供了新的微调路径，实际上通过高效算法提升了图像生成质量。未来工作可进一步探索方法在更多模型和复杂场景中的扩展应用，以增强泛化能力。

title: PromptLoop: Plug-and-Play Prompt Refinement via Latent Feedback for Diffusion Model Alignment
abstract: Despite recent progress, reinforcement learning (RL)-based fine-tuning of diffusion models often struggles with generalization, composability, and robustness against reward hacking. Recent studies have explored prompt refinement as a modular alternative, but most adopt a feed-forward approach that applies a single refined prompt throughout the entire sampling trajectory, thereby failing to fully leverage the sequential nature of reinforcement learning. To address this, we introduce PromptLoop, a plug-and-play RL framework that incorporates latent feedback into step-wise prompt refinement. Rather than modifying diffusion model weights, a multimodal large language model (MLLM) is trained with RL to iteratively update prompts based on intermediate latent states of diffusion models. This design achieves a structural analogy to the Diffusion RL approach, while retaining the flexibility and generality of prompt-based alignment. Extensive experiments across diverse reward functions and diffusion backbones demonstrate that PromptLoop (i) achieves effective reward optimization, (ii) generalizes seamlessly to unseen models, (iii) composes orthogonally with existing alignment methods, and (iv) mitigates over-optimization and reward hacking while introducing only a practically negligible inference overhead.

light_analysis.overview: PromptLoop提出一个基于潜在反馈的逐步提示优化框架，以插件式方式改进扩散模型的对齐。
light_analysis.motivation: 尽管强化学习在扩散模型微调中取得进展，但存在泛化能力不足、可组合性差和易受奖励黑客攻击的问题。现有提示优化方法多为前馈式，在整个采样轨迹中使用单一优化提示，未能充分利用强化学习的序列特性，导致对齐效果受限。因此，需要一种更灵活、鲁棒的模块化方法来解决这些挑战。
light_analysis.method: PromptLoop是一个插件式强化学习框架，通过引入潜在反馈实现逐步提示优化。核心创新在于训练一个多模态大语言模型，利用强化学习基于扩散模型采样过程中的中间潜在状态，迭代地更新提示。这种方法避免了直接修改扩散模型权重，实现了与Diffusion RL结构类似的对齐，同时保持了基于提示方法的灵活性和通用性。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果表明，PromptLoop在多种奖励函数和扩散骨干网络下均表现优异。它能有效优化奖励函数，无缝泛化到未见过的扩散模型，并与现有对齐方法正交组合，增强了系统的灵活性。此外，PromptLoop显著减轻了过优化和奖励黑客问题，同时仅引入微不足道的推理开销。这些优势通过广泛的对比实验验证。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究的主要贡献是提出了PromptLoop框架，有效解决了扩散模型对齐中的泛化和鲁棒性问题，展示了基于提示方法的潜力。学术上，它结合了强化学习和多模态语言模型，推动了模块化对齐技术的发展。实际应用中，可提升扩散模型的生成质量和安全性。未来工作可探索在更多任务中的应用和效率优化。

title: KnowledgeSmith: Uncovering Knowledge Updating in LLMs with Model Editing and Unlearning
abstract: Knowledge editing and machine unlearning are two popular approaches for large language models (LLMs) to stay up-to-date. However, the knowledge updating mechanism of LLMs remains largely unexplored due to insufficient, isolated, and small-scale evaluation. For instance, are LLMs similar to humans in modifying certain knowledge? What differs editing and unlearning as training data increases? This paper proposes KnowledgeSmith, a unified framework to systematically understand the updating mechanism of LLMs. We first cast editing and unlearning as instances of one constrained optimization problem. Then, we propose an automatic dataset generator that provides structured interventions across multiple graph levels and data scales, enabling controlled studies of how different modification strategies propagate through model knowledge. Extensive experiments demonstrate nuanced insights over knowledge propagation, plasticity scaling, consistency, and robustness. For instance, our results show that LLMs do not exhibit similar updating as humans for different levels of knowledge, and there exists consistency-capacity trade-off. We hope our findings can offer suggestions to the design of more reliable and scalable strategies. Code: https://github.com/AIFrontierLab/KnowledgeSmith.git

light_analysis.overview: KnowledgeSmith提出了一个统一框架，通过模型编辑和遗忘系统探究大型语言模型的知识更新机制。
light_analysis.motivation: 本研究的动机在于大型语言模型的知识更新机制仍未被充分理解，现有评估方法存在不足、孤立且小规模的问题，导致无法有效比较知识编辑和机器遗忘策略。这影响了模型在实际应用中保持知识更新的可靠性。该问题重要，因为随着模型规模扩大，高效更新知识对避免偏见和错误信息传播至关重要，而现有方法缺乏系统性分析来揭示不同策略的内在机制。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出KnowledgeSmith框架，核心方法首先将知识编辑和机器遗忘统一为一个约束优化问题，实现理论上的整合。然后，开发了一个自动数据集生成器，通过在多个图级别（如概念层级）和数据尺度上引入结构化干预，控制研究不同修改策略如何通过模型知识传播。关键创新在于提供系统性、可控制的实验设置，利用优化技术和数据生成技术来探究知识更新机制，从而克服了传统评估的局限性。
light_analysis.result: 主要实验结果表明，大型语言模型在不同知识级别上的更新方式与人类不同，并且存在一致性-容量权衡。研究提供了对知识传播、可塑性缩放、一致性和鲁棒性的细致洞察，揭示了模型更新行为的复杂性。例如，摘要未明确说明具体性能提升数据，但表明该方法能系统揭示机制。与基线方法的对比情况未在摘要中详细描述，但整体结果显示KnowledgeSmith框架提供了更全面的分析视角。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文的主要贡献是提出KnowledgeSmith框架，系统理解大型语言模型的知识更新机制，为设计更可靠和可扩展的知识更新策略提供建议。学术价值在于深入探究模型行为，推动知识编辑和遗忘的理论发展；实际应用价值在于改进模型维护和更新过程。局限性可能涉及实验规模和泛化能力，未来工作可进一步探索优化策略、大规模数据应用以及与其他模型的对比研究。

title: GeoSketch: A Neural-Symbolic Approach to Geometric Multimodal Reasoning with Auxiliary Line Construction and Affine Transformation
abstract: Geometric Problem Solving (GPS) poses a unique challenge for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), requiring not only the joint interpretation of text and diagrams but also iterative visuospatial reasoning. While existing approaches process diagrams as static images, they lack the capacity for dynamic manipulation - a core aspect of human geometric reasoning involving auxiliary line construction and affine transformations. We present GeoSketch, a neural-symbolic framework that recasts geometric reasoning as an interactive perception-reasoning-action loop. GeoSketch integrates: (1) a Perception module that abstracts diagrams into structured logic forms, (2) a Symbolic Reasoning module that applies geometric theorems to decide the next deductive step, and (3) a Sketch Action module that executes operations such as drawing auxiliary lines or applying transformations, thereby updating the diagram in a closed loop. To train this agent, we develop a two-stage pipeline: supervised fine-tuning on 2,000 symbolic-curated trajectories followed by reinforcement learning with dense, symbolic rewards to enhance robustness and strategic exploration. To evaluate this paradigm, we introduce the GeoSketch Benchmark, a high-quality set of 390 geometry problems requiring auxiliary construction or affine transformations. Experiments on strong MLLM baselines demonstrate that GeoSketch significantly improves stepwise reasoning accuracy and problem-solving success over static perception methods. By unifying hierarchical decision-making, executable visual actions, and symbolic verification, GeoSketch advances multimodal reasoning from static interpretation to dynamic, verifiable interaction, establishing a new foundation for solving complex visuospatial problems.

title: From Text to Talk: Audio-Language Model Needs Non-Autoregressive Joint Training
abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have attracted significant interest in extending their capabilities to multimodal scenarios, particularly for speech-to-speech conversational systems. However, existing multimodal models handling interleaved audio and text rely on autoregressive (AR) methods, overlooking that text depends on target-target relations whereas audio depends mainly on source-target relations. In this work, we propose Text-to-Talk (TtT), a unified audio-text framework that integrates AR text generation with non-autoregressive (NAR) audio diffusion in a single Transformer. By leveraging the any-order AR property of absorbing discrete diffusion, our approach provides a unified training objective for text and audio. To support this hybrid generation paradigm, we design a modality-aware attention mechanism that enforces causal decoding for text while allowing bidirectional modeling within audio spans, and further introduce three training strategies that reduce train-test discrepancies. During inference, TtT employs block-wise diffusion to synthesize audio in parallel while flexibly handling variable-length outputs. Comprehensive experiments on Audio-QA, ASR, AAC and speech-to-speech benchmarks show that TtT consistently surpasses strong AR and NAR baselines, with additional ablation and training-strategy analyses confirming the contribution of each component. We will open-source our models, data and code to facilitate future research in this direction.

title: Who to Trust? Aggregating Client Predictions in Federated Distillation
abstract: Under data heterogeneity (e.g., $\textit{class mismatch}$), clients may produce unreliable predictions for instances belonging to unfamiliar classes. An equally weighted combination of such predictions can corrupt the teacher signal used for distillation. In this paper, we provide a theoretical analysis of Federated Distillation and show that aggregating client predictions on a shared public dataset converges to a neighborhood of the optimum, where the neighborhood size is governed by the aggregation quality. We further propose two uncertainty-aware aggregation methods, $\mathbf{UWA}$ and $\mathbf{sUWA}$, which leverage density-based uncertainty estimates to down-weight unreliable client predictions. Experiments on image and text classification benchmarks demonstrate that our methods are particularly effective under high data heterogeneity, while matching standard averaging when heterogeneity is low.

title: TopoSculpt: Betti-Steered Topological Sculpting of 3D Fine-grained Tubular Shapes
abstract: Medical tubular anatomical structures are inherently three-dimensional conduits with lumens, enclosing walls, and complex branching topologies. Accurate reconstruction of their geometry and topology is crucial for applications such as bronchoscopic navigation and cerebral arterial connectivity assessment. Existing methods often rely on voxel-wise overlap measures, which fail to capture topological correctness and completeness. Although topology-aware losses and persistent homology constraints have shown promise, they are usually applied patch-wise and cannot guarantee global preservation or correct geometric errors at inference. To address these limitations, we propose a novel TopoSculpt, a framework for topological refinement of 3D fine-grained tubular structures. TopoSculpt (i) adopts a holistic whole-region modeling strategy to capture full spatial context, (ii) first introduces a Topological Integrity Betti (TIB) constraint that jointly enforces Betti number priors and global integrity, and (iii) employs a curriculum refinement scheme with persistent homology to progressively correct errors from coarse to fine scales. Extensive experiments on challenging pulmonary airway and Circle of Willis datasets demonstrate substantial improvements in both geometry and topology. For instance, $β_{0}$ errors are reduced from 69.00 to 3.40 on the airway dataset and from 1.65 to 0.30 on the CoW dataset, with Tree length detected and branch detected rates improving by nearly 10\%. These results highlight the effectiveness of TopoSculpt in correcting critical topological errors and advancing the high-fidelity modeling of complex 3D tubular anatomy. The project homepage is available at: https://github.com/Puzzled-Hui/TopoSculpt.

title: DART: A Server-side Plug-in for Resource-efficient Robust Federated Learning
abstract: Federated learning (FL) emerged as a popular distributed algorithm to train machine learning models on edge devices while preserving data privacy. However, FL systems face challenges due to client-side computational constraints and from a lack of robustness to naturally occurring common corruptions such as noise, blur, and weather effects. Existing robust training methods are computationally expensive and unsuitable for resource-constrained clients. We propose a novel data-agnostic robust training (DART) plug-in that can be deployed in any FL system to enhance robustness at zero client overhead. DART operates at the server-side and does not require private data access, ensuring seamless integration in existing FL systems. Extensive experiments showcase DART's ability to enhance robustness of state-of-the-art FL systems, establishing it as a practical and scalable solution for real-world robust FL deployment.

light_analysis.overview: 提出DART，一种服务器端插件，用于以零客户端开销增强联邦学习的鲁棒性。
light_analysis.motivation: 联邦学习（FL）作为一种分布式训练方法，在保护数据隐私的同时，面临客户端计算资源有限和对自然腐败（如噪声、模糊和天气效应）缺乏鲁棒性的挑战。这些挑战限制了FL在现实世界中的应用，尤其是在资源受限的边缘设备上。现有的鲁棒训练方法通常计算成本高，不适合FL环境，因此迫切需要一种高效、低开销的解决方案来提升FL系统的稳健性。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出DART（数据无关鲁棒训练）插件，该插件部署在服务器端，不访问客户端私有数据，从而以零客户端开销增强FL模型的鲁棒性。关键创新在于其数据无关性和服务器端操作，允许无缝集成到现有FL系统中。具体技术实现，如使用的算法或模型架构，摘要未明确说明，但强调了其兼容性和资源效率。
light_analysis.result: 通过大量实验，DART被证明能够有效增强state-of-the-art FL系统对自然腐败的鲁棒性。尽管摘要未提供具体的性能指标（如准确率提升百分比），但实验展示了DART在提升模型稳健性方面的能力，使其成为实用和可扩展的解决方案，适用于实际部署。与基线方法相比，DART在不增加客户端计算负担的情况下显著改善了FL系统的鲁棒性。
light_analysis.conclusion: DART的主要贡献在于提出了一种服务器端插件，以零客户端开销增强联邦学习的鲁棒性，解决了现有方法计算效率低的问题。这项研究具有重要的学术价值，为FL领域提供了新的鲁棒性增强思路，并在实际应用中促进了FL系统的部署。局限性可能包括对特定类型腐败的适应能力，未来工作可探索更多腐败场景或优化插件性能。

title: A Compression Based Classification Framework Using Symbolic Dynamics of Chaotic Maps
abstract: We propose a novel classification framework grounded in symbolic dynamics and data compression using chaotic maps. The core idea is to model each class by generating symbolic sequences from thresholded real-valued training data, which are then evolved through a one-dimensional chaotic map. For each class, we compute the transition probabilities of symbolic patterns (e.g., `00', `01', `10', and `11' for the second return map) and aggregate these statistics to form a class-specific probabilistic model. During testing phase, the test data are thresholded and symbolized, and then encoded using the class-wise symbolic statistics via back iteration, a dynamical reconstruction technique. The predicted label corresponds to the class yielding the shortest compressed representation, signifying the most efficient symbolic encoding under its respective chaotic model. This approach fuses concepts from dynamical systems, symbolic representations, and compression-based learning. We evaluate the proposed method: \emph{ChaosComp} on both synthetic and real-world datasets, demonstrating competitive performance compared to traditional machine learning algorithms (e.g., macro F1-scores for the proposed method on Breast Cancer Wisconsin = 0.9531, Seeds = 0.9475, Iris = 0.8469 etc.). Rather than aiming for state-of-the-art performance, the goal of this research is to reinterpret the classification problem through the lens of dynamical systems and compression, which are foundational perspectives in learning theory and information processing.

light_analysis.overview: 该论文提出了一种基于混沌映射符号动力学和数据压缩的分类框架，以动态系统和信息压缩视角重新解释分类问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 该研究旨在探索分类问题的替代方法，结合动态系统和信息理论视角。传统机器学习算法虽有效，但可能未充分利用符号表示和压缩原理，而动态系统和压缩在学习理论中具有基础地位。本文通过融合混沌映射和符号动力学，旨在解决如何以新颖方式重新诠释分类任务，强调模式识别中的信息编码和动态演化过程，以丰富分类方法的多学科交叉应用。
light_analysis.method: 该方法首先对实值训练数据进行阈值化处理，生成符号序列，并通过一维混沌映射演化这些序列。对于每个类别，计算符号模式的转移概率（例如在二返回映射中的`00`、`01`、`10`、`11`），并汇总统计形成类特定的概率模型。在测试阶段，测试数据同样被符号化，通过反向迭代（一种动态重构技术）使用类特定的符号统计进行编码。分类决策基于压缩表示的长度，选择最短表示的类别作为预测标签，体现了符号动力学、混沌映射和压缩学习的融合。
light_analysis.result: 在合成和真实数据集（如乳腺癌威斯康星、Seeds、Iris）上评估了提出的ChaosComp方法。宏观F1分数显示，在乳腺癌威斯康星数据集上为0.9531，Seeds数据集为0.9475，Iris数据集为0.8469。与传统机器学习算法相比，该方法展现出竞争性能，表明在多个数据集上实现了较高的分类准确率，尽管研究目标不是追求最先进水平，但验证了其在实践中的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究的主要贡献在于开发了一个基于混沌映射符号动力学和数据压缩的分类框架，为分类问题提供了动态系统和信息处理的新视角。它不仅展示了与现有方法的竞争性能，还重新诠释了分类任务，强调符号表示和压缩在学习理论中的基础作用。潜在的局限性可能包括混沌映射参数优化和计算效率，未来工作可探索更广泛的动态系统应用或改进编码策略，以增强方法的普适性和实用性。

title: When Brain Foundation Model Meets Cauchy-Schwarz Divergence: A New Framework for Cross-Subject Motor Imagery Decoding
abstract: Decoding motor imagery (MI) electroencephalogram (EEG) signals, a key non-invasive brain-computer interface (BCI) paradigm for controlling external systems, has been significantly advanced by deep learning. However, cross-subject MI-EEG decoding remains challenging due to substantial inter-subject variability and limited labeled target data, which necessitate costly calibration for new users. Many existing multi-source domain adaptation (MSDA) methods indiscriminately incorporate all available source domains, disregarding the large inter-subject differences in EEG signals, which leads to negative transfer and excessive computational costs. Moreover, while many approaches focus on feature distribution alignment, they often neglect the explicit dependence between features and decision-level outputs, limiting their ability to preserve discriminative structures. To address these gaps, we propose a novel MSDA framework that leverages a pretrained large Brain Foundation Model (BFM) for dynamic and informed source subject selection, ensuring only relevant sources contribute to adaptation. Furthermore, we employ Cauchy-Schwarz (CS) and Conditional CS (CCS) divergences to jointly perform feature-level and decision-level alignment, enhancing domain invariance while maintaining class discriminability. Extensive evaluations on two benchmark MI-EEG datasets demonstrate that our framework achieves average accuracies of 86.17% and 78.41%, outperforming a broad range of state-of-the-art baselines. Additional experiments with a large source pool validate the scalability and efficiency of BFM-guided selection.
comment: This work has been submitted to Elsevier for possible publication

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种基于脑基础模型和Cauchy-Schwarz散度的新框架，用于跨被试运动想象解码，实现动态源选择和判别性对齐。
light_analysis.motivation: 该研究旨在解决跨被试运动想象脑电图解码的挑战，主要问题是信号在个体间差异显著且目标数据标签有限，导致新用户需要昂贵的校准过程。现有多源域适应方法常常不加选择地融合所有源域，忽视了被试间的大变异性，这不仅会引发负迁移问题，还带来过高的计算成本。同时，这些方法过于关注特征分布对齐，却忽略了特征与决策级输出之间的明确依赖关系，从而限制了其保持判别结构的能力，因此亟需更有效的技术来提升解码性能和应用实用性。
light_analysis.method: 该论文提出一个新颖的多源域适应框架，核心是利用预训练的大型脑基础模型进行动态和信息化的源被试选择，以确保只有相关源参与适应过程，避免负迁移和降低计算开销。创新点在于联合使用Cauchy-Schwarz散度和条件Cauchy-Schwarz散度，同步执行特征级和决策级对齐，以增强域不变性并维持类判别结构。该方法在两个基准MI-EEG数据集上进行评估，涵盖了标准实验设置，确保了技术的可靠性和泛化性，未详细说明具体模型架构，但强调了BFM的预训练特性和CS散度的数学应用。
light_analysis.result: 在两个标准MI-EEG数据集上的广泛实验表明，该框架实现了平均准确率86.17%和78.41%，显著超越了一系列最先进的基线方法，证明了其性能优势。附加实验使用大源池进行验证，确认了脑基础模型引导源选择具有良好的可扩展性和高效性，在处理多源数据时展现出更低计算成本和更高适应性，有效支持了跨被试解码的实用化需求，尽管未提供基线方法的具体对比数据，但总体结果表明框架在准确性和效率上的改进。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究的主要贡献是提出了融合脑基础模型和Cauchy-Schwarz散度的新框架，成功解决了跨被试运动想象解码中的关键难题。学术上，它推动了多源域适应技术的发展，强调了动态源选择和判别性联合对齐的重要性，为脑电图分析提供了新思路；实际应用中，该框架有望提升脑机接口系统的用户友好性和效率，减少对新用户的校准需求，促进非侵入式控制的普及。未来工作方向可能包括扩展到其他脑电图任务、进一步优化模型参数，以及探索更多发散度方法的应用潜力，但摘要未明确说明具体局限性。

title: COALA: Numerically Stable and Efficient Framework for Context-Aware Low-Rank Approximation
abstract: Recent studies suggest that context-aware low-rank approximation is a useful tool for compression and fine-tuning of modern large-scale neural networks. In this type of approximation, a norm is weighted by a matrix of input activations, significantly improving metrics over the unweighted case. Nevertheless, existing methods for neural networks suffer from numerical instabilities due to their reliance on classical formulas involving explicit Gram matrix computation and their subsequent inversion. We demonstrate that this can degrade the approximation quality or cause numerically singular matrices.   To address these limitations, we propose a novel inversion-free regularized framework that is based entirely on stable decompositions and overcomes the numerical pitfalls of prior art. Our method can handle possible challenging scenarios: (1) when calibration matrices exceed GPU memory capacity, (2) when input activation matrices are nearly singular, and even (3) when insufficient data prevents unique approximation. For the latter, we prove that our solution converges to a desired approximation and derive explicit error bounds.

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出了一种基于稳定分解的无需逆运算的规范化框架，用于上下文感知低秩近似，以解决现有方法的数值不稳定性问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 上下文感知低秩近似在压缩和微调大规模神经网络中具有重要应用潜力，通过加权输入激活矩阵提升近似性能。然而，现有方法因依赖显式计算Gram矩阵及其逆而存在数值不稳定性，这可能导致近似质量下降或数值奇异矩阵，限制了方法的可靠性和扩展性。这一问题的重要性在于，稳定的近似对于高效处理现代深度学习模型的计算和存储需求至关重要，以支持网络压缩的实际应用。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出了COALA框架，一种无需逆运算的规范化方法，完全基于稳定的矩阵分解如QR或SVD来避免数值不稳定性。核心创新点在于避免了传统的显式Gram矩阵计算和求逆，转而采用分解技术处理挑战性场景，包括GPU内存超出容量、输入激活矩阵接近奇异或数据不足的情况。此外，论文推导了解收敛到期望近似的理论证明和显式误差界，增强了方法的理论基础，确保在复杂条件下提供有效近似。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确说明具体的实验结果数据，如准确率或效率提升的数值指标。但通过理论分析，论文证明所提框架能有效处理内存限制、矩阵奇异性和数据不足等挑战性场景，提供数值稳定的近似解。与现有方法相比，COALA避免了数值不稳定问题，提高了近似的可靠性。理论结果包括解收敛的证明和误差界推导，这为方法的数学有效性提供了支撑。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文的主要贡献是提出了一个数值稳定和高效的框架用于上下文感知低秩近似，解决了现有方法中的数值不稳定性问题。研究的学术价值在于改进了低秩近似的数学基础，提供了理论保证；实际应用价值在于可应用于神经网络压缩和微调，提升计算效率。潜在的局限性可能包括方法在不同数据集或模型架构上的泛化能力，未来工作可以扩展到更广泛的优化问题或与其他压缩技术结合以增强性能。

title: TimeRecipe: A Time-Series Forecasting Recipe via Benchmarking Module Level Effectiveness
abstract: Time-series forecasting is an essential task with wide real-world applications across domains. While recent advances in deep learning have enabled time-series forecasting models with accurate predictions, there remains considerable debate over which architectures and design components, such as series decomposition or normalization, are most effective under varying conditions. Existing benchmarks primarily evaluate models at a high level, offering limited insight into why certain designs work better. To mitigate this gap, we propose TimeRecipe, a unified benchmarking framework that systematically evaluates time-series forecasting methods at the module level. TimeRecipe conducts over 10,000 experiments to assess the effectiveness of individual components across a diverse range of datasets, forecasting horizons, and task settings. Our results reveal that exhaustive exploration of the design space can yield models that outperform existing state-of-the-art methods and uncover meaningful intuitions linking specific design choices to forecasting scenarios. Furthermore, we release a practical toolkit within TimeRecipe that recommends suitable model architectures based on these empirical insights. The benchmark is available at: https://github.com/AdityaLab/TimeRecipe.

light_analysis.overview: TimeRecipe 是一个时间序列预测基准框架，通过模块级评估系统揭示设计组件有效性并推荐模型架构。
light_analysis.motivation: 时间序列预测在金融、气象等领域应用广泛，但深度学习模型中设计组件（如序列分解、归一化）的有效性在不同条件下存在争议。现有基准主要评估整体模型性能，缺乏对模块级别组件的深入分析，导致难以优化模型设计和理解最佳实践。为了解决这一问题，本研究旨在通过模块级基准系统评估组件，以提供更具体的指导。
light_analysis.method: 本研究提出 TimeRecipe 框架，在模块级别系统评估时间序列预测方法的设计组件。关键创新包括进行超过 10,000 次实验，覆盖多种数据集、预测范围和任务设置，以测试如系列分解、归一化等组件的独立效果。框架使用统一基准，旨在揭示设计选择与预测场景之间的联系，并包含一个实用工具包。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果显示，通过 TimeRecipe 框架对设计空间进行详尽探索，可以生成模型在性能上超越现有最先进方法。例如，超过 10,000 次实验提供了实证数据，表明特定组件在不同条件下更为有效，与高层基准相比，模块级评估提供了更深入的直觉和优化指导。
light_analysis.conclusion: TimeRecipe 的主要贡献是提供了一个模块级基准框架和实用工具包，填补了现有基准在深入分析组件有效性方面的空白。它在学术上提升了时间序列预测模型设计的理解，并在实际应用中通过推荐系统促进模型优化。未来工作可扩展基准以涵盖更多组件和复杂场景，摘要未明确说明具体局限性。

title: EHR2Path: Scalable Modeling of Longitudinal Patient Pathways from Multimodal Electronic Health Records
abstract: Forecasting how a patient's condition is likely to evolve, including possible deterioration, recovery, treatment needs, and care transitions, could support more proactive and personalized care, but requires modeling heterogeneous and longitudinal electronic health record (EHR) data. Yet, existing approaches typically focus on isolated prediction tasks, narrow feature spaces, or short context windows, limiting their ability to model full patient pathways. To address this gap, we introduce EHR2Path, a multimodal framework for forecasting and simulating full in-hospital patient pathways from routine EHRs. EHR2Path converts diverse clinical inputs into a unified temporal representation, enabling modeling of a substantially broader set of patient information, including radiology reports, physician notes, vital signs, medication and laboratory patterns, and dense bedside charting. To support long clinical histories and broad feature spaces, we introduce a Masked Summarization Bottleneck that compresses long-term history into compact, task-optimized summary tokens while preserving recent context, improving both performance and token efficiency. In retrospective experiments on MIMIC-IV, EHR2Path enables next-step pathway forecasting and iterative simulation of complete in-hospital trajectories, while outperforming strong baselines on directly comparable tasks. These results establish a foundation for scalable pathway-level modeling from routine EHRs supporting anticipatory clinical decision-making. Our code is available at https://github.com/ChantalMP/EHR2Path.

light_analysis.overview: EHR2Path框架通过统一多模态电子健康记录数据和引入掩码总结瓶颈，实现了可扩展的纵向患者路径预测和模拟。
light_analysis.motivation: 预测患者病情演变（如恶化、恢复、治疗需求）对支持主动和个性化护理至关重要，但现有方法通常聚焦孤立预测任务、窄特征空间或短上下文窗口，限制了建模完整患者路径的能力。本研究旨在解决这一问题，以处理异质和纵向电子健康记录数据，提高临床决策的准确性和全面性。
light_analysis.method: EHR2Path是一个多模态框架，将多样化临床输入（如放射报告、医生笔记、生命体征）转换为统一时间表示，以建模广泛患者信息。关键创新是引入Masked Summarization Bottleneck，通过压缩长期历史为紧凑、任务优化的总结令牌，同时保留最近上下文，提高性能和令牌效率。该方法基于MIMIC-IV数据集，支持路径预测和迭代模拟。
light_analysis.result: 在MIMIC-IV数据集上的回顾性实验中，EHR2Path实现了下一步患者路径预测和完整医院轨迹的迭代模拟。在直接可比任务上，该方法优于强基线，展示了其有效性和可扩展性，但摘要未明确说明具体性能指标如准确率提升。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究为从常规电子健康记录中实现可扩展的路径级建模奠定了基础，支持预见性临床决策制定。其学术价值在于提出了统一表示和压缩机制，实际应用价值在于促进个性化护理。未来工作可扩展至更多医疗场景，并进一步验证局限性，摘要未明确说明具体限制。

title: Navigating the Latent Space Dynamics of Neural Models
abstract: Neural networks transform high-dimensional data into compact, structured representations, often modeled as elements of a lower dimensional latent space. In this paper, we present an alternative interpretation of neural models as dynamical systems acting on the latent manifold. Specifically, we show that autoencoder models implicitly define a latent vector field on the manifold, derived by iteratively applying the encoding-decoding map, without any additional training. We observe that standard training procedures introduce inductive biases that lead to the emergence of attractor points within this vector field. Drawing on this insight, we propose to leverage the vector field as a representation for the network, providing a novel tool to analyze the properties of the model and the data. This representation enables to: (i) analyze the generalization and memorization regimes of neural models, even throughout training; (ii) extract prior knowledge encoded in the network's parameters from the attractors, without requiring any input data; (iii) identify out-of-distribution samples from their trajectories in the vector field. We further validate our approach on vision foundation models, showcasing the applicability and effectiveness of our method in real-world scenarios.

light_analysis.overview: 本文提出将神经模型解释为潜在流形上的动力系统，利用隐式定义的向量场作为分析模型和数据的新工具。
light_analysis.motivation: 研究动机是解决神经网络模型行为理解不足的问题。现有方法多聚焦于静态表示，忽视了训练引入的动态偏置，这限制了分析模型泛化和记忆机制的能力，而这些问题对提升模型鲁棒性和解释性至关重要。通过探索潜在空间动力学，本研究旨在提供更深入的分析工具，以弥补现有方法的局限性，推动模型理论发展。
